title: Voter turnout
description: Practice your skills in this quiz on the factors impacting voter turnout

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a structural barrier to political participation enforced in certain states today? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Poll taxes
Literacy tests
Party-line voting
Voter ID laws

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which pairing below correctly matches both a constitutional protection of voting rights and a past or current structural barrier to voting? **

 | Constitutional protection of voting rights | Structural barrier to voting
- | - | -
**A** | The right to vote will not be denied  or abridged on account of sex | Rational choice
**B** | Literacy tests cannot be used as a voter qualification | Felony disenfranchisement
**C** | The right of citizens who are 18 years or older to vote cannot be denied | Poll taxes
**D** | The right to vote cannot be denied based on race, color, or previous condition of servitude | Party-line voting

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following policies is an example of state action to improve voter turnout? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Motor voter laws
Voter ID laws
Literacy tests
Poll taxes

assessment_items.question_content:
Voter turnout in the 2024 presidential election (Census Bureau)

| **Education Level**            | **Voter Turnout (%)** |
|---------------------------|-------------------|
| Less than high school     | 40%              |
| High school graduate      | 52.5%            |
| Some college              | 64%                 |
| Bachelor’s degree         | 77.2%            |
| Advanced degree           | 82.5%            |

**What best explains the lower voter turnout rates among Americans without a high school diploma?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: People in this group may lack access to voter registration opportunities.
People in this group are less likely to be affected by state-level election laws.
College graduates are legally required to vote in presidential elections.
People in this group tend to vote early or by absentee ballot.

assessment_items.question_content:
A state legislature passes a law that reduces the number of polling places in rural counties and shortens voting hours. During the next election, long wait times are reported at the remaining polling locations. A civic engagement group surveys residents in those areas and finds that many feel elected officials don’t represent their interests. Local news outlets also report a decline in voter registration in several rural counties.

**Which statement best explains why voter turnout is likely to decrease in rural areas of the state?**



[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Structural obstacles and low political efficacy both reduce citizens’ likelihood of voting.
Voters in rural areas tend to support third-party candidates, who are often excluded from ballots.
Political polarization in rural areas makes many voters withdraw from the political process.
Negative campaign advertising can discourage people from voting by making them feel disillusioned with politics.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d0b20051ac37fe0e07b3d7b4118b7255.png)

Source: [Statista.com](https://www.statista.com/statistics/245878/voter-turnout-of-the-exit-polls-of-the-2012-elections-by-ethnicity/)

**Which of the following statements is best supported by the data in the chart?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Race influences which candidate  voters support in an election
Black Americans are more likely to vote than any other race
Political party ideology has a more significant impact on elections than race
Voter demographics  have no impact on who wins an election

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the image below to answer the following question. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6360c9218898f7b44146d6b5743f0ecb.png)

Source: [United States Election Project](http://www.electproject.org/national-1789-present)

**Between the years 1984 and 2014, which of the following best represents the range of differences in voter turnout between each presidential election and the following midterm two years later?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Use the image below to answer the following question. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6360c9218898f7b44146d6b5743f0ecb.png)

Source: [United States Election Project](http://www.electproject.org/national-1789-present)

**Which of the following best explains why the two election turnout trends differ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Presidential terms are longer than that of members of Congress
Voters believe they have more at stake in local congressional elections than in presidential elections
Political parties spend more money getting out the vote in local congressional elections than on presidential campaigns
Presidential elections are better publicized with a national audience than midterm elections

assessment_items.question_content:
On a political survey, Patrick Yang reveals that he rarely votes, because he does not believe the federal government listens to or responds to his needs, nor does he think the average citizen has much influence over political affairs. 

**A political scientist would characterize these attitudes as representing which of the following? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Low political efficacy
Libertarianism
Political socialization
Retrospective voting

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the graph below to answer the following question. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dbead5e0c48b152191d058dd2536bbdc.png)

Source: [Pew Research Center](http://www.people-press.org/2017/05/03/public-trust-in-government-1958-2017/#iframe)

**Which of the following statements accurately describes a trend depicted in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Trust in government declined across all generations over the course of George H.W. Bush’s administration
Trust in government rose steadily across all generations throughout Clinton's first term
Trust in government fell most sharply among the greatest generation during the Carter administration
Boomers consistently showed the highest trust in government during the Reagan administration

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the graph below to answer the following question. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dbead5e0c48b152191d058dd2536bbdc.png)

Source: [Pew Research Center](http://www.people-press.org/2017/05/03/public-trust-in-government-1958-2017/#iframe)

**Which of the following statements best explains the significance of the generation group trends in the graph above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Downward trends in trust of the federal government among all generations between 1965-1980 indicate a corresponding decrease in citizen efficacy and health of US civil society
All generation groups show a similar level of trust in government across the George H.W. Bush and Clinton  administrations
All generations exhibit downward trends in trust of the federal government and corresponding decrease in citizen efficacy whenever a Democrat was serving as president
All presidential administrations exhibit declining public trust by the end of their terms, indicating this is a normal state of affairs

assessment_items.question_content:
Voter preferences by race and ethnicity in the 2024 presidential election (Census Bureau)

| Race/Ethnicity  | Donald Trump (R) (%) | Kamala Harris (D) (%) |
|-----------------|--------------------------|---------------------------|
| White           | 57%                      | 42%                       |
| Black           | 13%                      | 86%                       |
| Latino          | 46%                      | 51%                       |
| Asian           | 40%                      | 55%                       |
| Native American | 68%                      | 31%                       |
| Other           | 52%                      | 44%                       |

**Which statement best explains how race and ethnicity influenced voter choice in the 2024 presidential election?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Black voters, along with most other racial and ethnic minority groups, were most likely to support the Democratic candidate.
Voters across racial and ethnic groups supported the Republican candidate at similar rates.
White and Native American voters were least likely to support the Republican candidate.
Latino and Asian voters supported both candidates at similar rates, showing less variation in support than other groups.

assessment_items.question_content:
|| **Reasons Voters Support Hillary Clinton or Donald Trump** ||
 | Clinton voters (%) | Trump voters (%)
- | - | -
**Negative assessment of opponent** | $28$ | $28$
**Qualifications/Experience** | $31$ | $16$
**Issues/Policies** | $11$ | $23$
**Personal qualities** | $13$ | $15$
**Partisanship** | $12$ | $ 6$
**Want change** | $<1$ | $9$
**Other** | $2$| $2$
**No opinion** | $3$ | $2$

**Based on the results of the 2016 election shown in the table, which of the following reasons was the most cited reason that both Clinton and Trump voters chose their preferred candidate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Negative assessment of opponent
Policies supported by the political candidate
Personal qualities of the political candidate
Political ideology of the voter and the political candidate

assessment_items.question_content:
|| **Reasons Voters Support Hillary Clinton or Donald Trump** ||
 | Clinton voters (%) | Trump voters (%)
- | - | -
**Negative assessment of opponent** | $28$ | $28$
**Qualifications/Experience** | $31$ | $16$
**Issues/Policies** | $11$ | $23$
**Personal qualities** | $13$ | $15$
**Partisanship** | $12$ | $ 6$
**Want change** | $<1$ | $9$
**Other** | $2$| $2$
**No opinion** | $3$ | $2$

**Which of the following generalizations is best supported by data in the table?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The characteristics of the candidates can dictate who voters support in an election
Contemporary political issues are the most important factors influencing voter choice
Voters primarily choose candidates to support based on the candidate’s political ideology
Voters primarily choose candidates based on the policies in their platforms

assessment_items.question_content:
Percent (%) Voting for | Strong Democrat | Weak Democrat | Independent | Weak Republican| Strong Republican
- | - | -
**Obama (Democrat)** | $94$ | $84$ | $58$ | $21$ | $4$
**McCain (GOP)**| $6$ | $16$| $42$ | $79$ | $96$

**Which of the following best summarizes the data presented in the table for the 2008 presidential election? ** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Party identification is a strong predictor of how a person voted in an election
Independent voters are more likely to align with Republican candidates
Partisanship has no impact on voter choice in an election
Weak Democrats and Republicans voters are more likely to stay home than vote
